paper_title: Paleontological Analysis of Bone Remains from Fouvent-Saint-Andoche

methods.description: Process for collecting and identifying bone samples from historical and modern excavations to determine the faunal spectrum and characterize paleoenvironmental parameters.

steps.description: Collect bone samples from both the 1842 and 1989-1992 excavations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bone samples from historical and modern excavations

steps.description: Identify and classify bone remains to species, genus, or family level.
steps.searchable_summary: classify bone remains by species, genus, or family

methods.description: Measurement and analysis of bone dimensions to characterize paleoenvironmental parameters and identify specific species.

steps.description: Measure osteometrical variables following Von den Driesch (1976) protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: measure bone dimensions using Von den Driesch protocol

steps.description: Analyze measurements to identify peculiar species using specific measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze bone measurements to identify species

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including the use of Kruskal-Wallis test for comparing samples.

steps.description: Perform statistical analysis using R© and XLStat© software.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using R and XLStat

steps.description: Use Kruskal-Wallis test to compare more than two samples of different sizes.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Kruskal-Wallis test for sample comparison
